name: Turkey Tasso
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Skinned and boned turkey breast
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the sugar, granulated garlic, onion powder, ground red pepper, white pepper, black pepper, paprika, salt, and cumin. Mix well.
2. Place the turkey breast in a large glass bowl and sprinkle with 4 tablespoons of the seasoning mixture, coating it evenly.
3. Store the remaining seasoning mix in a tightly sealed container for future use.
4. Sprinkle the turkey with liquid smoke, rubbing the seasoning and liquid smoke over the entire breast.
5. Cover the bowl and marinate the turkey in the refrigerator for two (2) days, turning occasionally.
6. Light the fire in a charcoal water smoker, cover, and let the heat and smoke accumulate.
7. When the smoker is ready, place the turkey breast on a wire rack and smoke for 7 hours. Add water as needed and a few mesquite chips every hour or so.
8. Remove the turkey tasso from the smoker when it is done. It will be very dry, almost like jerky, which is normal.
9. Store the cooled turkey tasso in freezer bags for up to one month in the refrigerator or six months in the freezer.
10. Note: This product is not intended for making sandwiches. It is primarily used in gumbo, red beans, stuffings, and some pasta dishes. Its pronounced flavor makes it a suitable substitute for smoked ham hocks or similar ingredients. Many people enjoy it on its own.
